rationale: The cited 2007 JMKR study appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s core theory domain: both examine forms of formal control/monitoring (output vs process/behavior) and how relational context moderates governance outcomes. The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as reconciling opposing views on formal control effects and reactance, which aligns closely with the cited paper’s contribution of reconciling conflicting monitoring perspectives via social-contract contingencies, so this is not an obvious incidental mention.
